en.extract:
The joyful greenbul (Chlorocichla laetissima) or joyful bulbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in east-central Africa. Its natural habitats are boreal forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Taxonomy and systematics
The joyful greenbul was originally described in the genus Andropadus and later re-classified within Chlorocichla.


Subspecies
Two subspecies of the joyful greenbul are recognized:

C. l. laetissima - (Sharpe, 1899): Imatong Mountains, East African and northern Albertine rift montane forests
C. l. schoutedeni - Prigogine, 1954: southern Albertine rift montane forests


References


External links
Image at ADW

The '''joyful greenbul''' ('''''Chlorocichla laetissima''''') or '''joyful bulbul''', is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in east-central Africa. Its natural [[habitat]]s are boreal [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.<ref name="StevensonFanshawe2004">{{cite book|last1=Stevenson|first1=Terry|last2=Fanshawe|first2=John|title=Birds of East Africa: Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=jcN1OCKC-bQC&pg=PA322|date=December 1, 2004|publisher=A&C Black|isbn=978-0-7136-7347-0|pages=322–}}</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The joyful greenbul was originally described in the genus ''[[Andropadus]]'' and later re-classified within ''Chlorocichla''.

===Subspecies===
Two [[subspecies]] of the joyful greenbul are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-04-12}}</ref>
*  ''C. l. laetissima'' - <small>([[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1899)</small>: [[Imatong Mountains]], [[East African montane forests|East African]] and northern [[Albertine rift montane forests|Albertine rift]] montane forests
*  ''C. l. schoutedeni'' - <small>[[Alexandre Prigogine|Prigogine]], 1954</small>: southern Albertine rift montane forests
